text:
Legendary Football Coach Bobby Bowden has it all.
As the all-time winningest coach in Division I football history, Coach Bowden has statues erected in his honor and national charities and awards named after him.
Four generations of fans have heard his message and witnessed his results.
He built a successful career, climbed to the top of his field, and has a loving family.
It's what we all want.
And yet, Coach Bowden, who, as he puts it, is in the "fourth quarter" of his life, has a message for us all.
The success .
.
.
the influence .
.
.
the accolades .
.
.
the wins.
.
.
none of it matters if our lives are not rooted in faith.
God trumps our best hand.
He always wins.
Which is how it should be.
That is the wisdom he wants to share.
Let him tell you why faith and happiness are inseparable.
No matter your life circumstance, The Wisdom of Faith shows you what really matters - from the perspective of a man who has made the journey.
In his New York Times best-selling book, Coach Bowden looks back over eighty years and invites us to look forward with him into the future.

Con que contamos?

My dear friend Natalie Norton and her family have shaped my life so distinctly.
Nothing crazy out of the ordinary, but just being around them, being loved by them and sharing life with them has blessed my heart more than words can explain.
Today my heart is full of Natalie.
I wanted to do something for her.
Yes, these are blurry.
Yes these are screen shots from video clips.
But they are perfect.
They are love.
They are hope.
They are peace.
Nat and Richie your perseverance and trust in the Lord despite the most trying circumstances shows me love, hope and peace.
Thank you from the bottom of my heart.
Happy 3rd Birthday Gavin, your life has served and IS serving SUCH a purpose.
Beautifully done Gina!
Your work speaks for itself.
Hugs to Natalie and Richie and their family on Gavin's birthday today.
Hi G: the photos are perfect.
End of story.
What a truly wonderful friend you are.
Oh Gina.
I love you so much.
You are such a faithful, kind, loyal, supportive friend.
What did I do before I had you in my life?
I don't know what I would do without you .

This post comes from our contributor FP Naomi.
As spring takes hold, the art of the iced drink regains its importance.
We want a cold drink in our hand and the sunshine on our face.
After spending four months deprived of both, who can blame us?
So the ice gets poured into the cup, and then what?
How about a drink that no one would ever expect outside of a restaurant?
How about Thai Iced Tea?
We all love it, and it's so easy to make it yourself.
Enjoy a cold glass of this herbal and vanilla sweet treat - the healthy way!
1.
Add water and tea mix to a large pot.
Let simmer for ten minutes.
2.
Kill the heat and let the tea brew for another five minutes.
3.
Using a fine strainer, pour the liquid into a pitcher and filter out the tea leaves.
4.
White the tea is still hot, stir in honey & sugar until both dissolve.
7.
Fill a cup with ice, and pour the tea in to about the ¾ line.
8.
Top the drink off with coconut milk & a straw.
Serve, and enjoy!
Check out Naomi's blog Numie Abbot.
